zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby於1196年出生在英國爾利斯比時，他的父親Sir John de Hellesby22歲，母親Leuca de Mobberley18歲。他和Alice de Thornton至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1263年享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby于1196年出生在英国爾利斯比时，他的父亲Sir John de Hellesby22岁，母亲Leuca de Mobberley18岁。他和Alice de Thornton至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1263年享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Acton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, especially in Shropshire and adjacent counties, named Acton. Generally, these are from Old English āc ‘oak’ + tūn ‘settlement’. In Ireland the surname has been established in Connacht since the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby was born in 1196, in Helsby, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir John de Hellesby, was 22 and his mother, Leuca de Mobberley, was 18. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Alice de Thornton. He died in 1263, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby est né en 1196, à Helsby, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Hellesby, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Leuca de Mobberley, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Alice de Thornton. Il est décédé en 1263, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby 1196 in Helsby, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Hellesby 22 und seine Mutter Leuca de Mobberley 18. Er hatte mit Alice de Thornton mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1263 im Alter von 67 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby è nato il nel 1196 nel Helsby, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John de Hellesby, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Leuca de Mobberley, aveva 18 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Alice de Thornton. Morì nel 1263, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesbyが1196年にイギリスチェシュアイアヘルスビーで生まれた時，父Sir John de Hellesbyは22歳，母Leuca de Mobberleyは18歳であった。彼はAlice de Thorntonとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1263年に67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby(이)가 1196년 영국 체실르 헬스비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Hellesby(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Leuca de Mobberley(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Alice de Thornton(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1263년 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby nasceu em 1196, em Helsby, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Hellesby, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Leuca de Mobberley, tinha 18 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Alice de Thornton. Ele faleceu em 1263, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby родился в 1196 г. в местности хелсби, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John de Hellesby было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Leuca de Mobberley, 18. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Alice de Thornton. Он умер в 1263 г. в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby nació en 1196, en Helsby, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Hellesby, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Leuca de Mobberley, tenía 18 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Alice de Thornton. Murió en 1263, a la edad de 67 años.
